current_status: Arriving Tenerife early Sunday May 10 (estimated 04:00–06:00 CEST, updated from earlier 03:00–05:00 estimate). Ship will anchor offshore — docking refused after Granadilla port worker blockade threat. Tender-boat/inflatable-boat evacuation by nationality groups, operations scheduled to begin noon Sunday May 10 after ship's early-morning arrival, health checks, and repatriation flight coordination; weather window Sunday noon–Monday only (sea state worsens thereafter). WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus met Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez at Moncloa (15:00 GMT May 9) before flying to Tenerife to personally oversee the disembarkation alongside Spain's health and interior ministers at a joint command post. Tedros published formal message to the people of Tenerife (who.int, May 9), stating 'This is not another COVID' and assuring residents of low public risk. Netherlands Government (government.nl) published first official weblog May 9 — as flag state, the Dutch Ministry of Foreign Affairs is coordinating repatriation with RIVM, Dutch embassy Madrid, and SCOT rapid-deployment consular team on site in Tenerife. 14 Spanish nationals to transfer to Gómez Ulla UATAN unit, Madrid. UK chartering dedicated evacuation aircraft (~22 British nationals) equipped with oxygen and testing kits. CDC teams positioned at Tenerife and Nebraska NQU for American passengers. ECDC published dedicated Q&A page for public/clinicians (May 9). CDC published 'Current Situation' summary page and second press release (May 9). CDC issued HAN advisory alerting US physicians to potential imported cases. Three passengers medically evacuated May 6; one medevac aircraft diverted to Canary Islands (life-support electrical fault), replacement dispatched. Ship's doctor in Johannesburg ICU, condition improving per WHO. UKHSA confirms two British nationals confirmed: one (Martin Anstee) hospitalised in Netherlands, one hospitalised in South Africa. UK 45-day mandatory isolation for all returning British passengers.

cases.description: Dutch male, 68, onset of symptoms at sea. Suspected infection during Ushuaia shore excursion.
Dutch female, 65, spouse of first case. Symptom onset two days after partner.
German male, 57, cabin on same deck as index cases.
Crew member, 34, close contact with affected passengers.

Martin Anstee, 56, British retired police officer serving as expedition guide aboard MV Hondius. Attended same excursion in Ushuaia. Medically evacuated May 6 to the Netherlands for specialist treatment.

American male, 44, suspected human-to-human transmission on board. Lab-confirmed as 6th case per Argentine MOH Boletín Epidemiológico Nacional week 16 (published ~May 7–8 2026) and Sky News reporting 6 confirmed + 3 suspected.
Swiss male, MV Hondius passenger. Confirmed by Swiss Federal Office of Public Health; receiving treatment at University Hospital Zurich.

British national, MV Hondius passenger. Confirmed with hantavirus and hospitalised in South Africa (separate from Martin Anstee, case 5). Second confirmed British case from the outbreak. Sources: UKHSA GOV.UK, Al Jazeera, ITV News (May 8 2026).

evacuations.description: Three people medically evacuated from MV Hondius near Cape Verde: two sick crew members (including the ship's doctor) and one asymptomatic German passenger who had close contact with the deceased German national (case 3). One of two evacuation aircraft made an emergency diversion to the Canary Islands due to an electrical fault in a patient life-support system; a replacement plane was dispatched by Spain. Ship's doctor transferred to Johannesburg ICU — condition serious but improving as of May 8 per WHO update. Other evacuees transported to specialized hospitals in the Netherlands and Germany.

notable_contacts.description: KLM flight attendant from Haarlem hospitalized at Amsterdam UMC with suspected hantavirus after working KLM flight KL592 (Johannesburg–Amsterdam, Apr 25) on which a deceased MV Hondius passenger (Dutch female, 69) had briefly boarded before being asked to disembark due to illness. PCR and antibody tests confirmed NEGATIVE for Andes virus. No additional cases linked to the flight.

UKHSA confirms two British nationals from MV Hondius have been confirmed with hantavirus: Martin Anstee (case 5), hospitalised in the Netherlands, and a second British national hospitalised in South Africa. UKHSA has issued a 45-day mandatory isolation directive for all returning British passengers, with active monitoring and testing. Risk to the UK general public assessed as very low. Sources: UKHSA GOV.UK, ITV News.

Third British national with SUSPECTED hantavirus identified on Tristan da Cunha — the remote South Atlantic island (pop. ~250) where MV Hondius briefly called. This is the first suspected case among passengers who disembarked at an intermediate port rather than remaining aboard. The individual is being assessed by health authorities; Tristan da Cunha has no resident physician and extremely limited medical infrastructure. Sources: UKHSA GOV.UK, RTE, Al Jazeera, ITV News, Xinhua, Medical Xpress.

Singapore CDA confirms two residents who were aboard MV Hondius are isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases (NCID) and undergoing hantavirus testing. First: 67-year-old Singaporean male, arrived Singapore May 2, asymptomatic. Second: 65-year-old Singapore Permanent Resident male, arrived May 6, mild rhinorrhoea. Both were also on the same Johannesburg–Singapore flight as a confirmed case on Apr 25. If tests negative, 30-day quarantine from last exposure date with repeat testing before release. Risk to Singapore general public assessed as low.

Six US states confirmed monitoring residents: Georgia (2), Texas (2), New Jersey (2), Arizona (1), Virginia (1), and California (1) — total at least 9 individuals. New Jersey is the 6th state; its two residents were not aboard the ship but potentially exposed during air travel abroad. All individuals currently asymptomatic. Total US nationals aboard MV Hondius: 17. Sources: Yahoo News live blog, NJ DOH press release, CBS News.

CDC deploying two separate teams for American passengers: one team dispatched to the Canary Islands to meet the ship upon arrival and escort the 17 Americans aboard; a second CDC team positioned at the National Quarantine Unit (NQU) in Nebraska. Americans will be repatriated on a charter flight and placed in quarantine at the NQU — a federally funded facility co-located with the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit at University of Nebraska Medical Center. This represents the first activation of the NQU for a hantavirus event. Sources: CNN, ABC News live updates.

Canada confirms three individuals self-isolating across two provinces. Two are former MV Hondius passengers in Ontario who disembarked at Saint Helena on April 24 and are asymptomatic. A third individual in Quebec was not aboard the ship but is self-isolating after possible contact with an infected person during international travel; PHAC assessed their risk as not high. All three are under 30-day monitoring by provincial health authorities. Four Canadian nationals remain aboard MV Hondius; consular officials dispatched to Canary Islands. Sources: CBC News, Global News, CP24.

SUSPECTED mainland European community-linked case: 32-year-old Spanish woman hospitalised in Alicante with mild respiratory symptoms (coughing, general malaise). She was identified through Europe-wide health alert system after travelling on the same flight as a deceased Dutch MV Hondius passenger; she was seated two rows behind the victim, with only brief contact (the passenger had boarded for a short time). Announced by Spain's Secretary of State for Health Javier Padilla on May 8, 2026. This is the first suspected case in mainland Europe linked to a flight contact rather than direct ship contact. PCR results expected within 24–48 hours — classification remains SUSPECTED as of announcement. Sources: RTE, The Olive Press, The Local ES, Xinhua, Devdiscourse (May 8 2026).

Second flight-linked contact identified in Spain — woman based in Barcelona. She was initially missed during contact tracing because she switched seats during the same flight as the deceased MV Hondius passenger, making seat-manifest reconstruction inaccurate. Traced and confirmed asymptomatic on May 9. Under monitoring; not hospitalised and not classified as suspected. Source: The Spanish Eye, May 9 2026.

France confirms a French man is being monitored as a hantavirus 'contact case' after travelling on the same commercial flight (Saint Helena → Johannesburg) as a deceased MV Hondius passenger. The flight carried 82 passengers and six crew. The man is asymptomatic; classified as contact under monitoring, not as a suspected case. Source: France 24, The Peninsula Qatar (reported ~May 6–9 2026).

track.label: Tenerife, Canary Islands (May 10, est. 04:00–06:00 CEST — offshore anchor, evacuation by nationality groups from noon Sunday)
